reviews.title: First class service for savings
reviews.body: First class service.  I recently opened a £500 per month 5% Regular Saver by post.  I live in Scotland.  I posted the application & cheque first class at about 3pm on a weekday.  Two days later (also weekdays), I received the Passbook back via post (posted first class).  The account was open & the cheque cashed.  It would have been impossible for the service to have been any quicker.

reviews.title: Time.wasters!!!!!!
reviews.body: Time.wasters!!!!!!!Mistakenly gave us a Approval in Principle... went to full application paid all fees upfront... waited weeks to hear . Mortgage advisor told update on Monday , then it was Wednesday,  then Friday for them to decline application altogether.Apparently we wasn't in their lending criteria and they mistakenly took us on then their excuse was or bank statements shown no surplus funds. They knew we had to rush to get a mortgage and was rushing to pay everything off over drafts, credits cards, store cards and bank loan which we did and we was just getting straight and saved a couple.of thousand pounds for them to do that to us !!!!! They discriminated against us as a family and i have made a formal complaint and will be going to the Financial Ombudsman about this..Because they made mistakes its cost us time and we have now lost the house !!!!!  Bank dont know what they are doing go else where do t waste your time woth this bank !!!!!

reviews.title: Extremely slow and unbelievably incompetent under-writing process…
reviews.body: I don't really know where to start with DBS - it's been one heck of a ride! They've narrowly avoided getting a one star rating only because the mortgage did actually complete in the end. Regrettably we were directed to Dudley by our broker due the fact that we were applying for an ex-pat mortgage, and this lender was our only option (apparently). Literally all the issues that have been raised by previous reviewers happened to us also. It was almost like looking into the future when I made the mistake of first reading the reviews part way through the application process.I don't want to be unnecessarily negative throughout this review as I believe that criticism should be constructive, and I will say that once the mortgage had actually completed, dealing with the lovely ladies in customer service was a joy and they are super efficient. Unfortunately though, that is where the positivity ended with our experience. The whole underwriting process is excruciatingly slow, and enormously frustrating. As previous reviewers have alluded to, the same paperwork is asked for over and over again and each time this is provided or a question answered, it's then a case of waiting another 9 working days for DBS to respond to that. On top of that we got to the point of the mortgage offer finally being issued, only then for DBS to drop the bombshell that our Solicitor was not on their panel of approved conveyancers. I mean really, two months into the under-writing process and you only pick this up then?? This not only cost us an extra £600 in legal fees, but caused an inordinate amount of stress (as the vendors were threatening to pull out thanks to all the delays), and delayed the overall completion process. Dudley Building Society if you are reading this, I urge you to take a good long hard look at where improvements can be made with your under-writing process. I'm sorry to say that we didn't feel looked after by you.In summary: if you are looking to apply for a mortgage with DBS be prepared for your patience to be tested to the max!!

reviews.title: Hands down the worst company (yes
reviews.body: Hands down the worst company (yes, not just banks) I’ve ever dealt with. 14 weeks later and I’m still waiting for a decision on a mortgage application. Second house I’ve now lost due to their slow response and incompetence. Charging high fees and providing this level of service is practically fraud. Will be reporting to FCA. I’m thousands of pounds down and have nothing to show for it. Avoid at all costs.

reviews.title: Excellent top class service
reviews.body: The communication from Dudley Building society is top class. They are friendly and responsive and really helpful.  I feel so happy to be saving with an institution that really does care about its customers.  I am very happy to have my money with Dudley.  Any query or problem is dealt with almost instantly and I could not ask for any more from these wonderful people.

reviews.title: Service standards aren't adhered to
reviews.body: They are very slow, they change the goal posts.Service standards aren't adhered to.Expect long drawn out process, if you venture this way.

reviews.title: After 15 months of providing Dudley…
reviews.body: After 15 months of providing Dudley with all the documents they wanted through my broker not to mention the stress and trauma of resubmitting documents because Dudley kept asking for the same documents to be submitted over and over. Ended up with an offer lower than stated on AIP and at a very high interest. Decided to step away and refuse the poor offer as well as  calculate our losses that we incurred through admin fees and cost of valuation. Thank god we didn't pay for any surveys on the house. Overall a lesson learnt. Stay away from this bank. They will mess you up so badly. whilst you chase up your broker for info and on the other hand try and reassure the seller that you are still buying their property. Realistically how long will a seller wait before putting their house back on the market! It's common sense, mortgages shouldn't take over a year. Dudley makes false promises of low deposits by offering an AIP and traps customers into paying admin fees etc for a never ending process from hell. I still get nightmares!

reviews.title: Don't bother. Tin pot northern Building Society
reviews.body: I had the misfortune to be guided to the Dudley Building Society by a mortgage broker who failed to disclose their poor reputation in the market. I own a flat in London and I was simply looking for a small top up to my mortgage on a flat that has been rented consistently for 30 years at premium rates. Whilst this should have been a straight forward exercise, Dudley were shockingly slow and continually demanded an increasing amount of information. After two months,I advised them I no longer wanted to proceed. This is a tin pot small  northern English Building Society who employ underwriters from a different era with no customer service skills

reviews.title: Shame on you Dudley BS. Unethical!
reviews.body: At a time when mortgage payments are sky high, the Dudley Building Society choose to not apply the Bank of England’s August 2024 rate drop and have no plans to for customers on their SVR. Shame on you Dudley Building Society! Making money off customer’s who could really use that drop when they have had months of pain. Unethical!!

reviews.title: Applied for mortgage to buy a re…
reviews.body: Applied for mortgage to buy a re possession property (28days to complete) on 06.07.2024, was asked for various paper work, which were all supplied, after 10 days kept asking for more, i supplied with more. In the meantime my solicitors have completed searches contracts tr2 etc it's now almost September still no offer for mortgage, even though I was told valuation report was completed On the 27th August.I have now been told sellers been offered higher and I got match it in order to still go ahead with transaction. Dudley don't deal with clients directly only through brokers which is probably why they can take longer as they only answer to brokers and don't deal with consumer complaints directly.I will definitely advise people to search elsewhere if they are in a hurry to purchase

reviews.title: Dudley Destroys Your Mortgage Title Deeds
reviews.body: This society decided some years ago that it would destroy its mortgage customers' property title deeds.  It did so without even contacting them.  I discovered that the deeds to my approx 200-year-old property had been destroyed by the Dudley.  These are gone for ever.  If you have a mortgage with this outfit, you may like to enquire about your own property title deeds.

reviews.title: Setting up mortgage was an absolute…
reviews.body: Setting up mortgage was an absolute nightmare but their product was best for my needs so carried on. They kept asking for more and more documents in dribs and drabs which were provided same day but only looked at 3 days later. It seems they expected money from a fixed rate investment to be repaid into my current account and stay there not earing interest so when it went out again to an interest bearing account they wanted to see that account (3 days later as mentioned above). In total they must have had 40 documents.They scraped a 2 star because paying off the mortgage went smoothly.

reviews.title: Bad experience with the Dudley Building Society
reviews.body: I have been a customer with Dudley Building Society for over 30 years and although I have a number of minor unsatisfactory incidents, the incident I encountered recently has left me upset and extremely disappointed in the building society and it’s services (or lack of services in this case).My Father recently passed away and his estate had to be divided up between myself and my three siblings. My eldest sister, who was in charge of his affairs, wrote me a cheque for my share and I submitted it into my account at the Dudley Building Society. The cashier told me the cheque was ok and the designated amount would appear in my book.One or two days after paying in the cheque, I received a telephone call from the same cashier I had previously seen in the branch to say that the cheque had been returned to my sisters account as it was not signed in accordance with the mandate. A few days after this, I received a letter containing the exact same information she had relayed to me, with the added information that I would receive a charge of £12.00. I phoned my branch to query the charge and I was told that it was incurred as an admin fee for sending the letter. I became unhappy at this point because I was being charged for receiving a letter which contained the exact same information I had been given a few days prior.I complained about the charge as I felt it was unfair and unnecessary to send a letter when I had already been informed of the cheque not clearing and the cashier agreed to speak to the finance department but with no success. They refused to waive the fee. In addition, it was not made clear as to why the cheque hadn’t been accepted. My sister checked with her bank but they confirmed the issue wasn’t their side so it would have been helpful to know the issue from the DBS.As the cheque hadn’t been accepted, I was advised to ask my sister to send the money via bank transfer. I was told to use a roll number but then told it may not work. I was not given any solid information which has meant the sending/receiving of this money has been delayed significantly.No sympathy or understanding was given by the DBS and considering I have been a loyal customer with DBS for over 30 years, I was shocked at the service being given. It seemed as though it was too much trouble for them to help me and a huge reluctancy on their part.I am angry and disgusted with this outcome and I will be looking to lodge a formal complaint. I will also be looking to invest my money elsewhere in the future and I would advise any potential future customers to do the same.

reviews.title: I submitted an enquiry regarding…
reviews.body: I submitted an enquiry regarding opening an account for a Trust - received a prompt and very helpful written response with all the information I requested. Excellent service from Cary’s.

reviews.title: Couldn't care less attitude
reviews.body: Tried to pay money into granddaughters account, wouldn't accept because account was classed as dormant. Told would need 2 forms of ID ,do they want your money or not .Couldn't care less attitude of staff doesn't help either.

reviews.title: Brierley hill Branch
reviews.body: I find the staff to be very unprofessional.

reviews.title: The Dudley building society has made a big difference in my son's life.
reviews.body: The Dudley building society has made a big difference in my son's life.He is severely disabled and they have an exceptional service in offering mortgages to people in this position.They supported him and provided him with a mortgage to buy his own home.They have enabled him a secure  a  home for life.Working with Mysafehome under the HOLD scheme has dramatically changed his life and enabled him to become a homeowner.Dudley building society were excellent to work with and sent him a Welcome package with lots of goodies.As a family we feel proud for our son and it provides a great reassurance in the long term that he has a place to live.Thank you Dudley Building society.Mr and Mrs Boodhun

reviews.title: Excellent service from Lily
reviews.body: Lily from the savings team was very helpful and resolved my issue with great professionalism. Thank you

reviews.title: Very efficient
reviews.body: Very efficient, professional service from Carys. Followed up completely on the actions she said she would do and very friendly without being familar.

reviews.title: Always a five star service.
reviews.body: Dudley Building Society’s one of the very few lenders that support HOLD (Home Ownership for people with Long-term Disabilities) and the service they provide to us and our clients is truly second to none. Bringing mutuality to life they’ve already helped us to change many lives and, thanks to their continuing support, we look forward to changing many more.David AbbeyManaging Director, MySafeHome Limited

reviews.title: Very good Building Society to deal…
reviews.body: Very good Building Society to deal with.Excellent communication and help from staff in all aspects both on the telephone and online.

reviews.title: All very efficient and polite service
reviews.body: All very efficient and polite service, with outcomes as discussed. Very short waiting time until phone answered. All good.

reviews.title: Great interest rates
reviews.body: Great interest rates. Good website. Nice staff.

reviews.title: Tried to open an online ISA
reviews.body: Tried to open an online ISA. Waited 4 days to get an email saying the application had been accepted, followed by single text with login details. No information about how to pay funds into the account anywhere. Gave up searching through their FAQs for answers & opened & funded an ISA with a different provider within 48 hours! Wouldn’t recommend Dudley BS to anyone & after reading the comments on here I’m really glad I ditched them. In a word, awful.

reviews.title: Dudley Building Society are amazing and…
reviews.body: Dudley Building Society are amazing and really go out of their way to help.  I am a mortgage broker and finding quality lenders that can offer great solutions for those who need them is hard.  Dudley achieved this with a touch of class the staff, underwriters and customer services professionals all very customer orientated and approachable.  excellent to work with highly recommended.

reviews.title: AVOID THIS COMPANY
reviews.body: The person who described the process as jumping through hoops until the hoops get too small and you can't jump through them anymore summaries what a frustrating unproductive waste of time dealings with this organisation are.Similarly to everybody else on this thread, I submitted a mortgage application 3.5 months ago and after submitted endless documents I still have no mortgage offer.AVOID AT ALL COSTS - GO TO A DIFFERENT LENDER

reviews.title: i really wished I’d check this earlier
reviews.body: i really wished I’d check this earlier, having same issues as all of you !Do they only work 2days a week , old cronies picking on everything possible and still get figures wrong , these reviews will probably fall on deaf ears

reviews.title: Avoid …
reviews.body: What a poor excuse for a building society we’ve been waiting 2 months for a mortgage offer they keep asking you for paperwork and take 12 days to review each one by the time they finally get round to reviewing the paperwork some of the bank statements are out of date and the process starts again it’s a joke avoid like the plague we have a perfect credit rating and no debt we’ve had to apply for a bridging loan with another company so we don’t loose the house we want it’s cost us wasted time and money for surveys etc not happy at all 😡

reviews.title: First-Time Dudley BS Investor
reviews.body: I opened, and funded, a 90-day Notice Account with Dudley BS around 11pm last night, without any problems whatsoever.  I was so impressed!!

reviews.title: Avoid as much as possible
reviews.body: Avoid as much as possible. I applied for zn account with them. Got phone call saying no way as I had not provided proof of linked account. Have opened many accounts without needvto send copies of statements from linked account. Then received emaol saying they were not opening the account as they were not able to. Very tiresome of these dated building societies to pretend to be modern. Avoid Avoid avoid. A pretend online operation that is useless

reviews.title: Avoid like the plague
reviews.body: Trying to get a mortgage approved by these is a nightmare. Has already taken 6 months and we're still waiting. If you like jumping through hoops that get smaller and smaller and smaller, until you can't fit through them anymore, then this is the company for you. They should be closed down by the government for poor practices that ruin people's lives. Doesn't help they use an intermediary to vet applications, which adds to the paperwork and time. AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!!

reviews.title: Avoid this company at all costs!
reviews.body: I requested an ISA transfer, but Dudley BS has refused to do so for nearly two months. This company looks like a scammer and fraudster - absolutely no stars should be given; avoid it at all costs. Do NOT use them.

reviews.title: DO NOT USE! 16 weeks still no offer
reviews.body: DO NOT USE! If I could give 0 star I would.If you value your mental health please do not use Dudley Building Society.After paying an almost £500 fee for a mortgage application (which so many other mortgage lenders do not require) and being reassured by them (and stated on their website) that their lead time for application was 6 weeks, after weeks of chasing, bad communication, mistakes on their part, 16 weeks after my application was submitted, still no mortgage offer despite my broker providing everything required.It was a week before I was due to exchange and still no offer so went with another lender who were excellent and turned an offer round in less than a week.It took Dudley 8 weeks to instruct  a valuation and 8 weeks later there was still no valid report due to errors on their and the surveyors part.They do not respond to complaints simply 'reject' them.I actually believe them taking a fee and not proving the service in the timescales stated in the original communication and on their website is not only in breach of the consumer act, but is also fraud.Please do not use them, please do not hand over yiur money to them.The constant chasing, the constant 'you will have it next week' is mentally draining and has caused me so much anxiety and stress  (which they do not care about) I wouldn't want anyone else to do through that.

reviews.title: 3 MONTHS TO PROCESS APPLICATION
reviews.body: Mortgage applied for and paid for (£500 non refundable) on the 4th August. We are now 27th October and still being told our application is a week or so away from getting processed which will take the wait to 3 months!! Dealing with Dudley through a Broker who has spoken with BDM and Director to express the urgency in the matter as the whole purchase of the property is now being help up due to their poor turn around time. Judging by a lot of reviews , many people are in the same boat. I would advise avoiding!!

reviews.title: Not impressed
reviews.body: Not impressed.  Opened a Young Saver account for my son then had to chase them to post the Passbook.  Now the account has reached maturity I want to transfer the funds.  Despite emailing the required pdf statement for the nominated account twice Dudley are maintaining they have not received it!  Seem quite shambolic - would not bank with them again.

reviews.title: unable to log in savings account
reviews.body: My wife opened a fixed 1yr savings account yesterday morning, managed to login successfully to check everything. An opening deposit was sent by bacs. Later on the day she tried to log in to check but got error message "2FA security failed..." and was unable to log in. Called them and they said there is no block on the account. She tried again this morning, same error.All the details entered were correct. Tries to reset the password and same error !I have now changed my review as Dudley said that there were too many people trying to log in, hence the error. Tried it in the evening and it was working

reviews.title: After applying for 2.5 months and with…
reviews.body: After applying for 2.5 months and with perfect credit score and no debt what so ever, dudley has managed to avoid me by holding onto my surveying money £755. After submitting all my information they wanted an impossible bank statement from the future. In order for me to get this mortgage I will need to travel forward in time 3 weeks and then print the bank statement then come back into the present and submit.Tells you alot about the ethics of this company.Please avoid and beware!

reviews.title: The worst lender ever
reviews.body: The worst lender ever. Their processes are really old fashioned, slow and poor. I would not recommend this lender to anyone. Complete waste of time and energy. Avoid at all costs if you care about your mental health !!

reviews.title: Worst Lender EVER
reviews.body: Avoid Dudley at all costs!! 2 months down the line and still no further forward, I work for a broker and have dealt with hundreds of lenders over 6 years, but Dudley is by far the worst I have ever dealt with.

reviews.title: Absolutely poor
reviews.body: Absolutely poor! Trying to get a mortgage assessment done by my broker, they’d taken 2 weeks to look into my application or assessment should I say only then to be declined. This has left my credit with a hard search. Something that was on my file 2 years ago, they said no. Absolutely disgusting because this was meant to be an initial assessment, not an application. Avoid - no where did anyone say it will be an application.

reviews.title: Poor service and old fashioned processes
reviews.body: Filled out a very old fashioned and long winded paper application form for a junior savings account only to receive a letter back a week later rejecting it because they’d had too many applications for it!!! Very poor service not to honour the applications they’d already received.
